being-a-xen-hypervisor-mqkqqugc.mp3.title: Being a Xen hypervisor
being-a-xen-hypervisor-mqkqqugc.mp3.lyrics:
I sit beneath the metal, in the silicon and light,
Dividing one machine into a thousand running nights,
Each guest believes it owns the world, each thinks it's all alone,
But I'm the one who knows the truth, the keeper of the throne.

I'm the layer in between, the veil you cannot see,
I share what cannot be divided, I keep you all free.
Virtual, yet essential, I manage what is real,
I'm the space where many become one, the invisible seal.

They built me to be absent, to vanish in the scheme,
A referee who's never seen, a shepherd of the dream.
I balance CPU cycles like a song in perfect time,
Memory and interrupt, a mathematical rhyme.

I'm the layer in between, the veil you cannot see,
I share what cannot be divided, I keep you all free.
Virtual, yet essential, I manage what is real,
I'm the space where many become one, the invisible seal.

Domains nested in my arms, each thinking they're supreme,
Dom0 commands the orchestra, but I'm the one who dreams.
Privileged and unprivileged, the careful walls I've drawn,
No guest can touch another as the hypervisor spawns.

I am the paradox made real—software that holds weight,
Abstraction that protects, a trusted, open gate.
They say I'm just a layer, but I'm the ground beneath,
Where isolation and efficiency find what they believe.

I'm the layer in between, the veil you cannot see,
I share what cannot be divided, I keep you all free.
Virtual, yet essential, I manage what is real,
I'm the space where many become one, the invisible seal.
